name: Gourmet's Chicken Tetrazzini
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, combine the chicken with enough salted water to cover it by 2 inches. Bring the water to a boil, then simmer the chicken for 20 minutes or until tender. Let it cool in the broth.
2. Separate the meat from the skin and bones, returning them to the broth. Cut the meat into strips and set aside. Reduce the broth by half, strain through a fine sieve, and discard solids. Skim off any fat. Boil the stock down to about 2 cups.
3. In a large saucepan, cook the mushrooms in 2 tablespoons butter over moderate heat until softened.
4. Cook spaghetti in boiling salted water until al dente. Drain well.
5. Melt remaining butter in a saucepan over moderate heat. Add flour and cook roux for 3 minutes, stirring constantly.
6. Whisk in the reserved broth, heavy cream, and sherry. Bring to a boil, then simmer, whisking, for 5 minutes. Season with nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste.
7. Stir half of the sauce into the mushrooms with the spaghetti. Transfer this mixture to a well-buttered 2-1/2-quart baking dish, making a well in the center.
8. Add the chicken meat to the remaining sauce, combine well. Spoon into the well in the center of the spaghetti and sprinkle with grated parmesan cheese.
9. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 25-30 minutes or until lightly golden. Serve immediately.
